publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 19, 2023 at 02:00

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is unprocessed. Contact the Medical Historical Library to request access.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred to the Medical Historical Library by William Summers, 2023.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sofia Simmonds (1917-2007) was a biochemist whose research interest was the metabolism of amino acids and peptides in the bacteria Escherichia coli. Simmonds graduated from Barnard College, then attended Cornell University, completing her PhD in 1942. She began working at Yale University in 1945 as a researcher and worked her way up to become a full professor of biochemistry in 1975. In 1969 she became professor of molecular biophysics and biochemistry after the department was reorganized. In 1988 she served as associate dean of Yale College. Simmonds and her husband Joseph Fruton co-authored the first comprehensive biochemistry textbook: General Biochemistry , which was published in 1953.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sofia Simmonds papers. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The papers include materials created by Sofia Simmonds (1917-2007) circa 1943-1995. Simmonds was a biochemist who trained at Cornell University, then spent her career working at Yale University. Simmonds and her husband Joseph Fruton co-authored the first comprehensive biochemistry textbook: General Biochemistry , which was published in 1953. The papers include correspondence, writings, grant applications, committee records, biochemistry department papers, and laboratory notebooks.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
